description_long: This house features a traditional timber-framed structure with a steeply pitched gable roof and a prominent central section. The exterior walls are characterized by exposed wooden beams and infill panels, giving it a classic half-timbered appearance. The front includes a covered entryway with a visible door and a small porch area, while the side reveals an attached garage or outbuilding with a sloped roof. Windows are arranged in symmetrical rows, especially on the upper story, and the roof is finished with dark shingles. The house sits on a grassy plot, and the overall design suggests a blend of rustic and historical European influences. The structure appears to be two stories tall, with the main living area elevated above the ground level.
description: A two-story, timber-framed house with a steep gable roof, symmetrical window rows, and a covered entryway, set on a grassy plot.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway, covered porch, and central gable roof with visible timber framing.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the attached garage or outbuilding and the extended roofline.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the symmetrical arrangement of windows and the full width of the gable roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the end of the house and the side of the garage or outbuilding.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered entryway with a single door, small porch, and exposed timber framing
